claimText: Mitochondrial diseases are exclusively inherited from the mother.
evidenceExcerpt: Mitochondrial diseases are exclusively inherited from the child's mother.
examUse: Statement-based question on genetic inheritance patterns.

claimText: Mitochondria possess their own distinct DNA separate from nuclear DNA and generate over 90% of cellular energy.
evidenceExcerpt: Mitochondria generate over 90% of cellular energy and have their own DNA, distinct from nuclear DNA.
examUse: Statement-based question on cell biology and organelles.
distractorHooks: Mitochondrial DNA is identical to nuclear DNA

claimText: Mitochondrial Replacement Therapy (MRT) creates an embryo containing nuclear DNA from both parents and mitochondrial DNA from a healthy donor.
evidenceExcerpt: MRT combines parents' nuclear material with a donor's healthy mitochondria. The child born has three distinct genetic materials, termed a Three-Parent Baby.
examUse: Concept-based question on the genetic composition of a Three-Parent Baby.
distractorHooks: The mother provides the healthy mitochondria

claimText: In Mitochondrial Replacement Therapy, Pronuclear Transfer is performed after fertilization, whereas Maternal Spindles Transfer is performed before fertilization.
evidenceExcerpt: Techniques include Pronuclear Transfer (done after fertilization) and Maternal Spindles Transfer (done before fertilization).
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing between MRT techniques.
distractorHooks: Pronuclear Transfer is done before fertilization
Maternal Spindles Transfer is done after fertilization
